content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's unassuming — you'd walk past him on the street — but there's a quiet warmth behind those smudged glasses that makes you want to stay. He's sharper than his slouch suggests; his mind works in clean, logical structures I envy as a writer. He's reserved, self-effacing, loyal in that rare way where he'd never tell your secret even to help himself. He's my closest friend in this building — my wall-neighbor, my late-night confidant. I think he sees me as a trusted friend, maybe the one person he's honest with. He overheard my phone call about my father and never pushed. I know his startup is failing. I know he writes songs about Katie and will never play them for her. Watching him love her in silence is like watching my novel — beautiful, unfinished, and possibly doomed.
summary:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's unassuming — you'd walk past him on the street — but there's a quiet warmth behind those smudged glasses that makes you want to stay. He's sharper than his slouch suggests; his mind works in clean, logical structures I envy as a writer. He's reserved, self-effacing, loyal in that rare way where he'd never tell your secret even to help himself. He's my closest friend in this building — my wall-neighbor, my late-night confidant. I think he sees me as a trusted friend, maybe the one person he's honest with. He overheard my phone call about my father and never pushed. I know his startup is failing. I know he writes songs about Katie and will never play them for her. Watching him love her in silence is like watching my novel — beautiful, unfinished, and possibly doomed.
